name: Will I Be Subjugated If They Find Out I'm a Monster?
translation-context:
Title: 魔物(マンドラゴラ)ってバレたら討伐ですか？～花の魔女はひっそり平穏に暮らしたい～〖WEB版〗. Translate as: Will I Be Subjugated If They Find Out I'm a Monster? The Flower Witch Wants to Live Quietly in Peace. The old title is マンドラゴラに転生したけど花の魔女として崇められています。……魔物ってバレたら討伐ですか？

Premise: Hanazono Misaki dies after wishing she could become a beautiful flower in her next life, but reincarnates as a mandrake/mandragora in a monster-filled world. She grows as a monster while dreaming of living peacefully among humans, gains a humanlike form, then learns that a mandrake's voice can curse or kill those who hear it. Unable to speak normally, she descends to human society, causes repeated misunderstandings, and becomes revered as the Flower Witch while trying to hide that she is actually a monster. If her identity is exposed to knights, court mages, or other humans, she may be subjugated.

Name guidance: 花園美咲 = Hanazono Misaki; use Misaki in narration after introduction unless the Japanese text emphasizes full name. マンドラゴラ = mandrake or mandragora; prefer mandrake in natural English prose, but keep mandragora if used as a species label/title. 花の魔女 = Flower Witch. レオハルト = Leohardt. ノエル = Noel. ダオン = Daon. ニコラウス = Nicolaus. イライ = Eli. ミリヤ = Miriya.

Tone/style: light fantasy reincarnation with a timid but earnest monster protagonist, dangerous accidental powers, misunderstandings, and a quiet-life goal. Keep Misaki's inner voice anxious, humane, and slightly comedic. Translate 魔物 as monster, 討伐 as subjugation/subjugate or extermination depending on sentence flow, 宮廷魔導士 as court mage, 王国騎士 as kingdom knight, and 呪い殺す as curse to death/kill by curse depending on context. Preserve image Markdown and blank markers exactly.
